title: Aluminum-alloy member and combined working method thereof
abstract:
PURPOSE: To obtain an Al-alloy member excellent in mechanical properties and dimensional accuracy, by forming an Al alloy having specified compositional ranges into a near-final shape by increasing a forging ratio in the required part and then by subjecting the resulting forged stock to finish forming by partially changing the forging ratio.
CONSTITUTION: The Al alloy has a composition principally consisting of, by weight, 4.0W7.5% Si, 0.2W1.2% Mg, ≤0.5% Cu, and the balance essentially Al. By increasing the forging ratio in the part requiring strength and resistance to pressure and by reducing the forging ratio in the part other than the above, this Al alloy is forged into a stock of near-final shape, which is maintained at about 300W500°C and subjected to finish forging by an impression method. In this way, the Al-alloy member in which internal defects are removed and which has complicated shape can be obtained.

claims:
[Claim 1]
an aluminum alloy casting and finish forging of the forging stock material close to the final shape, as by forging, casting a forged material has excellent mechanical properties and dimensional accuracy over, casting defects within the stock and aluminum alloy member
[Claim 2]
weight % of the component with an aluminum alloy member, Si: 4.0 - 7.5%, mg: 0.2 - 1.2%, cu: ≦ 0.5% and the balance substantially aluminum alloy member is first described in claim 1 characterized Al
[Claim 3]
% by weight aluminum alloy member components, Si: 4.0 - 7.5%, mg: 0.2 - 1.2%, cu: ≦ 0.5% Ti and: 0.1 - 0.2%, Sr:0.01 - 0.05%, Ti Cr:0.04 - 0.35% range, Sr, 1 kind of Cr, which has 2 or more components, and the balance substantially aluminum alloy member is first described in claim 1 characterized Al
[Claim 4]
the rough forging and finish forging stock casting forged material after the final shape of each part by partially changing the size ratio of the corresponding member, and an overall shape of a shape close to the final shape of the stock material as by casting, finishing after forging impression only by a forging process, a portion of the forging ratio larger than needed strength and resistance, and other portions of the forging ratio small as 0 or, the forging ratio of the shape of the aluminum alloy member is formed partially in place of the final finishing of the composite method
[Claim 5]
in the first described in claim 4 characterized in that the forging ratio of 5 - 65% aluminum alloy member composite machining method
[Claim 6]
weight % of the component is an aluminum alloy member, Si: 4.0 - 7.5%, mg: 0.2 - 1.2%, cu: ≦ 0.5% and the balance substantially in said second and third 5 claim 4 wherein Al of aluminum alloy member
[Claim 7]
the aluminum alloy member by weight component %, Si: 4.0 - 7.5%, mg: 0.2 - 1.2%, cu: ≦ 0.5% Ti and: 0.1 - 0.2%, Sr:0.01 - 0.05%, Ti Cr:0.04 - 0.35% range, Sr, 1 kind of Cr, which has 2 or more components, wherein the first and the balance substantially in claim 4 5 wherein third Al aluminum alloy member of the composite method
